mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and builder demand, which forces equity markets lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a positive 60-day forward return in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, which reliably pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months where INDPRO rises during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.0% annualized growth rate, inflation fears trigger immediate equity sell-offs as investors anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL annualized growth breaches the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate simultaneously increases indicates labor force participation expansion that dilutes wage growth and suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase signals peak economic momentum that precedes corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 accelerates during late-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the industrial production index reflect broad-based economic strength that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with rising industrial production.
